assumptions: Per-contract Greeks use European BSM assumptions when enrichment is required.
Aggregate directional scores combine OI, volume, IV surface, and Greeks with fixed transparent weights.
Positioning window 'near' applies horizon damping to the aggregate bias score.
limitations: Positioning signals are heuristic aggregates and not execution advice.
Low liquidity or sparse strikes can reduce reliability for skew, pin, and flow metrics.
BSM-based mispricing and ITM probabilities depend on quoted implied volatility quality.

references.title: Carr and Wu (2009), Variance Risk Premiums
De Fontnouvelle et al. (2003), Option Mispricing
Avellaneda and Lipkin (2003), Pin Risk and Probability Models

analyst_summary: For SPY, the aggregate options surface leans bullish — more call-heavy OI and flow. That reflects overall market positioning in the chain, not a specific actor; heavy clusters can sometimes line up with large books, but we do not label institutions. Model confidence is 60% for the next 1-2 weeks horizon.

signals.name: Call Open Interest Share
signals.explanation: A larger share of call OI points to aggregate upside interest in the chain — sometimes consistent with sizable call interest, but not exclusive to any one player.

signals.name: Put/Call OI Ratio
signals.explanation: Elevated put/call OI can indicate hedging pressure or defensive positioning.

signals.name: Intraday Options Flow (Calls Share)
signals.explanation: Call-heavy volume relative to puts may indicate bullish tactical flow.

signals.name: Gamma Tilt
signals.explanation: Positive gamma tilt can support stabilization and upside continuation.

signals.name: Dollar Call Flow Share
signals.explanation: Dollar call volume ÷ total dollar volume — economic magnitude of flow, not just contract counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

signals.name: Dollar Put/Call Open Interest
signals.explanation: Dollar-weighted put/call OI ratio — magnitude-weighted positioning vs simple counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

positioning.name: Call Open Interest Share
positioning.explanation: A larger share of call OI points to aggregate upside interest in the chain — sometimes consistent with sizable call interest, but not exclusive to any one player.

positioning.name: Put/Call OI Ratio
positioning.explanation: Elevated put/call OI can indicate hedging pressure or defensive positioning.

positioning.name: Intraday Options Flow (Calls Share)
positioning.explanation: Call-heavy volume relative to puts may indicate bullish tactical flow.

positioning.name: Gamma Tilt
positioning.explanation: Positive gamma tilt can support stabilization and upside continuation.

positioning.name: Dollar Call Flow Share
positioning.explanation: Dollar call volume ÷ total dollar volume — economic magnitude of flow, not just contract counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

positioning.name: Dollar Put/Call Open Interest
positioning.explanation: Dollar-weighted put/call OI ratio — magnitude-weighted positioning vs simple counts (Bollen & Whaley 2004; Pan & Poteshman 2006).

volatility.name: ATM Implied Volatility · IV Rank
volatility.explanation: ATM IV is 18.00% (percentage points). Intra-chain IV rank 33% — how that ATM print ranks versus all implied volatilities in this snapshot.

volatility.name: 25-Delta Skew
volatility.explanation: IV of the ~25-delta put minus the ~25-delta call (nearest expiry). Positive skew often reflects downside protection demand. Wing richness (butterfly) and 10Δ skew extend the surface read (Carr & Wu 2009; Bates 2000).

volatility.name: Term Structure
volatility.explanation: Need two expirations with ATM IV to infer term structure.

flow.name: Unusual Activity Score
flow.explanation: Counts strikes where volume is elevated versus open interest (ratio > 2, volume > 500), scaled into a 0–100-style intensity score.

flow.name: Aggregate Volume / OI
flow.explanation: Total options volume divided by total open interest — higher values can indicate more tactical trading versus stuck inventory.

flow.name: Dominant Flow Moneyness
flow.explanation: Delta-bucketed open interest and dollar volume — shows whether flow concentrates at the money or in wings.  Dominant call bucket atm; dominant put bucket atm (delta bands).

gamma.name: Net Gamma Exposure
gamma.explanation: Sum of gamma × OI × 100 × spot for calls minus puts — a coarse dealer-gamma proxy.

gamma.name: Gamma Regime
gamma.explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

gamma.name: Gamma Flip Strike (nearest expiry)
gamma.explanation: Strike where cumulative per-strike GEX (calls − puts, OI-weighted, ×100×spot) crosses zero on the front expiry — a common “flip” narrative (SqueezeMetrics-style dealer gamma; Knuteson 2021, arXiv:2006.00975). No cumulative GEX sign change on the front expiry — flip strike unset.

gamma.name: Net Delta Exposure (DEX)
gamma.explanation: Sum of delta × open interest × 100 (calls + puts) — aggregate directional exposure in the chain (Lakonishok, Lee & Poteshman 2007; OCC/CBOE-style positioning summaries).

gamma.name: Vanna Exposure
gamma.explanation: Net vanna exposure — when negative, rising IV forces dealers to sell delta, amplifying downside moves. When positive, IV spikes trigger buying. Classified regime: neutral.

gamma.name: Charm Exposure
gamma.explanation: OI-weighted charm (∂Δ/∂T): positive net charm often means delta drifts up with time, so dealers may lean into selling at the open (overnight drift). Overnight delta drift: neutral.

structure.name: Max Pain Strike
structure.explanation: Strike that minimizes total intrinsic value paid to option holders at expiration (nearest expiry only) — a common magnet narrative, not a forecast.

structure.name: Strike Magnets (OI)
structure.explanation: Largest open-interest strikes in the nearest expiration — where inventory tends to cluster.

structure.name: Implied Move (Straddle)
structure.explanation: ATM straddle mid / spot for the nearest expiration — a rough implied move proxy; when DTE is available we also show Brenner & Subrahmanyam (1988) σ from the straddle and DTE-consistent daily/period moves. DTE=7, σ_BS88≈3.19% ann.; daily≈0.2012%, horizon≈0.5323% (see implied_move_detail).

regime_explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

scenarios.label: Bullish continuation
scenarios.narrative: Upside continuation if SPY holds above option-supported levels.

scenarios.label: Bearish unwind
scenarios.narrative: Downside pressure if hedging demand rises and calls unwind in SPY.

scenarios.label: Range-bound
scenarios.narrative: Mean-reverting regime likely if neither side gains flow dominance.
